Per-Strategy Annual Projection

Extrapolated from 5.3-year BINANCE backtest (Dec 2020 → Apr 2026), scaled to live sizing: $50 margin × 10x = $500 notional (vs $1000 backtest). Scale factor 0.50× applied. Assumes steady regime — real returns will deviate.

Strategy Symbol TF PF Trades/Yr Annual % (Backtest) Projected Annual $ Max DD

Disclaimer

These projections are not guarantees. Profit factor and trade counts come from historical BINANCE data and assume the bot's filters match the TradingView Pine backtest exactly. Variables that can cause divergence: exchange slippage/funding/latency, regime changes (2020-2024 had strong trends), strategies running simultaneously on finite capital (8 concurrent max), and tax/withdrawal timing. Treat the ranking as more reliable than the absolute $.

Open Whale Positions

Live positions opened by the whale-tracking bot. Exits trigger on SL, TP, or smart-money signal flip.

Coin Symbol Direction Entry Qty SL TP Signal Type Margin
No open whale positions.

Latest Whale Signal Scan

Last scan at 2026-05-31T01:15:26.740205+00:00. DIVERGENCE = smart vs rekt opposite (higher conviction, 1.5× size). CONSENSUS = ≥80% smart-money agreement. Crowded-trade + edge-decay filters applied.

Coin WEEX Symbol Signal Direction Confidence Smart Long% Smart Short% #Traders Funding Liq Risk / Fuel Recency Reasoning
ETH ETHUSDT CONSENSUS_SHORT SHORT 10/10 8% 92% 12 +1.4% — — smart short 92% (12 wallets) · net $-138.7M · uPnL $+8,449,385
TON TONUSDT CONSENSUS_SHORT SHORT 10/10 12% 88% 8 +1.4% — — smart short 88% (8 wallets) · net $-20.1M · uPnL $+2,855,424
LINK LINKUSDT CONSENSUS_SHORT SHORT 7/10 14% 86% 7 +1.4% — — smart short 86% (7 wallets) · net $-4.2M · uPnL $+741,101

How this bot trades

Source: Hyperliquid public API (leaderboard + clearinghouseState). 15-min poll cadence.
Signal: Smart-money basket (top 20 by all-time PnL) vs rekt basket (worst 20 monthly, $1k+ accounts). A coin classifies as DIVERGENCE when ≥70% of smart money and ≥70% of rekt sit on opposite sides. It classifies as CONSENSUS when ≥80% of smart money agrees, regardless of rekt.
Filters: min 5 smart-money traders per coin · smart basket must be winning on the coin (edge-decay guard) · skip if both smart AND rekt are crowded on the same side · coin must be listed on WEEX.
Sizing: Consensus $500 notional ($50 × 10x), Divergence $750 ($75 × 10x). Stops: 1.5× ATR(14,4H), Targets: 3.0× ATR (2R reward:risk). Early exit if smart-money consensus drops below 55%.
Caveat: Whale edge decays. Expect 30–80% Y1, 10–30% Y2, 0–15% Y3 without re-tuning. Retune thresholds quarterly.

Open Funding-Fade Positions

Positions opened by the funding-fade bot. Exits trigger on SL, TP, time-stop (8h), or funding-normalize back into the 25–75th pctile band.

Coin Symbol Direction Entry Qty SL TP Funding @ Entry Margin
No open funding-fade positions.

Latest Funding-Fade Signal Scan

Last scan at never. Signals fire when current funding rate hits 97th-percentile of its 30-day distribution AND magnitude ≥ 0.05%/8h. Entries only inside the ±30min window around 00:00 / 08:00 / 16:00 UTC funding fixings. Filters: low-vol ATR regime, trend not opposing the fade, OI ≥ $20M, top-100 market cap.

Coin Symbol Direction Confidence Funding Pctile OI Filters Reasoning
No funding signals yet — waiting for first 1h poll (~15-day warmup unless HL history cache has populated).

How this bot trades

Source: Hyperliquid /info fundingHistory + metaAndAssetCtxs (free public API). 1-hour poll cadence with 30-day rolling history per symbol cached 12h.
Signal: 97th-percentile funding rate (top OR bottom) + 0.05%/8h absolute floor. When funding is extreme, retail is overcrowded on one side and the basis with spot widens; mean-reversion of funding is the edge.
Filters: trade only in ±30min window around the 3 daily funding fixings · low-vol regime (ATR < ATR_SMA, FLIPPED vs whale bot) · trend doesn't oppose the fade · OI ≥ $20M · top-100 market cap · per-coin 8h cooldown.
Sizing: $25 margin × 10x = $250 notional (half-size during validation). Lifts to $500 after ≥10 closed trades with positive expectancy.
Exits: 2.5×ATR stop, 1.5×ATR target (asymmetric — fast reversion or fail), 8h time-stop (one funding cycle), funding-normalize exit if rate moves back into the inner 25-75th band.
Caveat: Funding-fade is regime-dependent. In strong trending markets, funding can stay extreme for days while price keeps trending. Low-vol filter helps but isn't a panacea.
